question: In the example outbound REST connector template, a user sets `REST Method` to `GET` and the `Request Body` field disappears from the properties panel. What in the template causes this behavior?

options.text: The Webhook ID field's pattern constraint disallows bodies on GET requests.
The `Request Body` property has `condition: { property: "httpMethod", oneOf: ["patch", "post", "delete"] }`, so it is only shown when one of those methods is selected.
GET methods cannot carry any binding; the modeler strips properties without a binding at deploy time.
The modeler automatically hides any optional property whenever a form is partially filled.

a.text: Webhook IDs are not part of the outbound REST template — that is an inbound webhook concept. Visibility here is controlled by a `condition` clause, not a Webhook ID pattern.

b.text: The `Request Body` entry in the template carries `condition: { property: "httpMethod", oneOf: ["patch", "post", "delete"] }`. With `httpMethod = get` the condition is false, so the modeler hides the property.

c.text: The `Request Body` property does have a `zeebe:input` binding to `body`; visibility is governed by the `condition` clause, not by binding presence.

d.text: The modeler does not auto-hide optional fields based on form completeness; visibility comes from the explicit `condition` clauses on each property.

explanation: Element templates use a `condition` clause to control which properties appear in the modeler's properties panel. In the REST connector template, the `Request Body` field is declared with `condition: { property: "httpMethod", oneOf: ["patch", "post", "delete"] }`. When the user picks `GET`, the condition does not match, so the field is hidden. The same conditional pattern is used for the Basic and Bearer authentication fields, which only show when the matching `authenticationType` value is selected.
